prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_complaint_record investigation_1)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint investigation_1 investigator_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(flag_product_for_validation investigation_1 product_batch_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record investigation_1 reviewer_1)
(mark_investigation_ready_for_change_package investigation_1 product_batch_1 reviewer_1)
(release_reviewer_from_record investigation_1 reviewer_1)
(register_complaint_record capa_1)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint capa_1 investigator_2)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(flag_impacted_process_for_validation capa_1 process_line_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record capa_1 reviewer_1)
(assign_reviewer_and_mark_capa_ready_for_package capa_1 process_line_1 reviewer_1)
(release_complainant_contact capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(assemble_change_control_package investigation_1 capa_1 product_batch_1 process_line_1 change_control_package_1)
(mark_change_control_package_validation_ready change_control_package_1 investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(finalize_investigation_record investigation_1 change_control_package_1)
(finalize_capa_record capa_1 change_control_package_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint investigation_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_investigation_in_quality_system investigation_1 investigator_1 regulatory_reference_1)
(release_complainant_contact investigation_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint capa_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_capa_in_quality_system capa_1 investigator_2 regulatory_reference_1)
(release_complainant_contact capa_1 complainant_contact_1)
(release_reviewer_from_record capa_1 reviewer_1)
(register_complaint_record casefile_1)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint casefile_1 investigator_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(attach_validation_protocol_to_casefile_and_package casefile_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1)
(complete_technical_review_without_validation_dependency casefile_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_equipment_to_casefile casefile_1 equipment_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record casefile_1 reviewer_1)
(initiate_validation_specialist_workflow casefile_1 equipment_1 reviewer_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1)
(link_validation_specialist_to_casefile casefile_1 validation_specialist_1)
(validation_specialist_complete_casefile_review casefile_1 validation_specialist_1 validation_protocol_1 change_control_package_1)
(finalize_casefile_without_release_authority casefile_1)
(release_complainant_contact casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(release_reviewer_from_record casefile_1 reviewer_1)
(unlink_validation_specialist_from_casefile casefile_1 validation_specialist_1)
(unlink_equipment_from_casefile casefile_1 equipment_1)
(register_complaint_record casefile_2)
(assign_investigator_to_complaint casefile_2 investigator_3)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(complete_complaint_triage casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(attach_validation_protocol_to_casefile_and_package casefile_2 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1)
(complete_technical_review_without_validation_dependency casefile_2 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_equipment_to_casefile casefile_2 equipment_1)
(assign_reviewer_to_record casefile_2 reviewer_1)
(initiate_validation_specialist_workflow casefile_2 equipment_1 reviewer_1 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1)
(link_validation_specialist_to_casefile casefile_2 validation_specialist_1)
(validation_specialist_complete_casefile_review casefile_2 validation_specialist_1 validation_protocol_2 change_control_package_1)
(finalize_casefile_without_release_authority casefile_2)
(release_complainant_contact casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint casefile_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_casefile_in_quality_system casefile_1 investigator_1 regulatory_reference_1)
(release_complainant_contact casefile_1 complainant_contact_1)
(link_complainant_contact_to_complaint casefile_2 complainant_contact_1)
(link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint casefile_2 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1)
(register_casefile_in_quality_system casefile_2 investigator_3 regulatory_reference_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for a pharmaceutics complaint to quality system domain. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Interpreting the Problem  
- The task involves creating a planning problem in PDDL for a pharmaceutics complaint to quality system domain.  
- The goal is to output an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line.  

## Formatting the Output  
- Each action must follow the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...), with no additional text.  
- The output should focus solely on the sequence of actions without including domain or problem definitions.

## Examining Record Relationships  
- Complaint records include subtypes like investigation_record, capa_record, and quality_case_file, all sharing common properties.  
- Casefile_1 and casefile_2 both reference investigation_1, capa_1, and change_control_package_1, but link to different validation protocols and suppliers.  

## Assessing Initial Conditions  
- Supplier_1 is available for casefile_1, while supplier_2 for casefile_2 is not initially available.  
- Resources like validation_specialist_1, equipment_1, reviewer_1, and investigators 1,2,3 are available, along with complainant_contact_1 and supporting_document_1.  

## Outlining Finalization Steps  
- Finalizing investigation_1 requires change_control_package_1 to be compiled and validation_ready, with similar steps for capa records.  
- Registration process involves finalizing records, linking regulatory references, and then registering, with various assemble actions needed for compilation.  

## Detailing Opening Process  
- To open records, they must first be registered, assigned an investigator, linked to complainant_contact_1, and triaged.  
- Only one complainant_contact_1 is available, potentially limiting linkage to multiple records like investigation_1, capa_1, casefile_1, and casefile_2.

## Extending Opening Process  
- Opening records like investigation_1 and capa_1 involves registering, assigning investigators, linking complainant_contact_1, and triaging.  
- Only one complainant_contact_1 is available, requiring shared linkage across multiple records like investigation_1, capa_1, casefile_1, and casefile_2.  

## Planning Record Finalization  
- Finalizing investigation_1 and capa_1 requires compiling change_control_package_1 and ensuring validation_ready, with shared package use across casefiles.  
- Technical review for casefiles depends on compiled package and validation_ready, necessitating prior processing of investigation and capa records.  

## Outlining Initial Actions  
- Start by registering investigation_1 with (register_complaint_record investigation_1), then assign investigator_1 with (assign_investigator_to_complaint investigation_1 investigator_1).  
- Link complainant_contact_1 to investigation_1 using (link_complainant_contact_to_complaint investigation_1 complainant_contact_1), followed by triaging with (complete_complaint_triage investigation_1 complainant_contact_1).  

## Detailing Validation Steps  
- Flag product_batch_1 for validation with (flag_product_for_validation investigation_1 product_batch_1 complainant_contact_1) after ensuring record_open and linked contact.  
- Assign reviewer_1 to investigation_1 with (assign_reviewer_to_record investigation_1 reviewer_1) to proceed, requiring record_open and available reviewer.

## Managing Regulatory References  
- Regulatory references can be reused across multiple records, becoming available again after registration, like regulatory_reference_1 for investigation_1.  
- Linking regulatory_reference_1 to investigation_1 with (link_regulatory_reference_to_complaint investigation_1 regulatory_reference_1 complainant_contact_1) prepares it for quality system registration.  

## Registering Investigation and CAPA  
- Register investigation_1 in the quality system with (register_investigation_in_quality_system investigation_1 investigator_1 regulatory_reference_1), freeing investigator_1 and regulatory_reference_1.  
- For capa_1, release complainant_contact_1 from investigation_1 with (release_complainant_contact investigation_1 complainant_contact_1), then link to capa_1 and register similarly.  

## Handling Casefile Opening  
- Release complainant_contact_1 from capa_1 with (release_complainant_contact capa_1 complainant_contact_1) to make it available for casefile_1.  
- Start opening casefile_1 by registering with (register_complaint_record casefile_1), then assign investigator_1 and link complainant_contact_1.

## Managing Casefile Resources  
- Unlink equipment_1 from casefile_1 with (unlink_equipment_from_casefile casefile_1 equipment_1) to make it available for reuse.  
- Release complainant_contact_1, reviewer_1, and validation_specialist_1 from casefile_1 using respective commands to free resources for casefile_2.  

## Opening Casefile 2  
- Register casefile_2 with (register_complaint_record casefile_2), then assign investigator_3 and link complainant_contact_1.  
- Complete triage and technical review for casefile_2 using validation_protocol_2 and change_control_package_1, skipping supplier review.  

## Finalizing Casefile Processes  
- Link equipment_1 and validation_specialist_1 to casefile_2, then finalize without release authority using (finalize_casefile_without_release_authority casefile_2).  
- Ensure all steps, including validation specialist review, are completed before finalization.  

## Registering Casefiles in Quality System  
- Release complainant_contact_1 from casefile_2 and relink to casefile_1, then link regulatory reference for registration.  
- Register casefile_1 in the quality system, releasing investigator_1 and regulatory reference, and repeat for casefile_2 using investigator_3.

## Reviewing Casefile Prerequisites  
- Casefile assembly after investigation and CAPA readiness doesn’t require complainant contact, allowing assembly before releasing contact from CAPA.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
